stem: Two Pauli operators commute when reordering them leaves the product unchanged. Which of the following pairs COMMUTE? Select all that apply.
code:
from qiskit.quantum_info import Pauli
# e.g. Pauli('XX').commutes(Pauli('ZZ'))

explanation.correct: Two multi-qubit Paulis commute when they anticommute on an EVEN number of qubits. AÔŐŚB and CÔŐŚD commute iff (an even count of the single-qubit factor pairs anticommute). XÔŐŚX vs ZÔŐŚZ: X,Z anticommute on both qubits (even) -> commute. XÔŐŚZ vs ZÔŐŚX: anticommute on both (even) -> commute. IÔŐŚX vs ZÔŐŚI: factors act on disjoint qubits, no anticommuting pair -> commute.

distractors.B: XÔŐŚI vs ZÔŐŚZ: they anticommute only on qubit 1 (X vs Z), an ODD count, so the pair anticommutes.
distractors.E: Single-qubit X and Z anticommute (XZ = ÔłĺZX), so they do not commute.
